title: Generators and lazy pipelines deterministic draft bank

publication.changeSummary: Misconception MCQ, canonical fill-gap, and runnable core task for Generators and lazy pipelines; every item remains blocked from formal exams pending independent human review.

items.title: Generators and lazy pipelines: reject the misconception
items.prompt: Which statement is source-aligned for Python: Beginner to Intermediate and corrects this misconception? Calling a generator function executes it immediately and the returned generator can be iterated repeatedly from the beginning.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted draft item; independent human source, technical, pedagogy, accessibility, and answer-oracle review is required before formal-exam eligibility.

hints: Compare each option with both the language-specific boundary case and the cited operational model.

feedback.correct: Correct. A generator body runs lazily during one-pass consumption and preserves suspended state; create a new generator for another traversal and close owned resources deliberately.
feedback.incorrect: That choice preserves the misconception. A generator body runs lazily during one-pass consumption and preserves suspended state; create a new generator for another traversal and close owned resources deliberately.

criteria.description: Selects the source-aligned correction and rejects the documented misconception.

privateAuthorNotes: A human reviewer must verify source fidelity and ensure the distractor remains plausible without teaching it as fact.

options.text: A generator body runs lazily during one-pass consumption and preserves suspended state; create a new generator for another traversal and close owned resources deliberately.
Calling a generator function executes it immediately and the returned generator can be iterated repeatedly from the beginning.

answer.explanation: A generator body runs lazily during one-pass consumption and preserves suspended state; create a new generator for another traversal and close owned resources deliberately.

items.title: Generators and lazy pipelines: canonical checkpoint
items.prompt: Complete the source-aligned Python: Beginner to Intermediate correction with the exact canonical phrase used by this lesson.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted draft item; independent human source, technical, pedagogy, accessibility, and answer-oracle review is required before formal-exam eligibility.

hints: Retrieve the short checkpoint phrase from the operational correction, then read the complete sentence for semantic fit.

feedback.correct: Correct. The checkpoint is 'one-pass consumption'. A generator body runs lazily during one-pass consumption and preserves suspended state; create a new generator for another traversal and close owned resources deliberately.
feedback.incorrect: The phrase must preserve the exact language rule. A generator body runs lazily during one-pass consumption and preserves suspended state; create a new generator for another traversal and close owned resources deliberately.

criteria.description: Supplies the exact canonical checkpoint phrase that completes the source-aligned correction.

privateAuthorNotes: Human reviewer must verify the accepted phrase and whether additional spelling variants are technically equivalent.
items.template: A generator body runs lazily during [[checkpoint]] and preserves suspended state; create a new generator for another traversal and close owned resources deliberately.

answer.explanation: A generator body runs lazily during one-pass consumption and preserves suspended state; create a new generator for another traversal and close owned resources deliberately.

items.title: Generators and lazy pipelines: runnable core task
items.prompt: Read a positive chunk size followed by words. Use a generator to emit lazy chunks and print chunks joined with vertical bars.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted draft item; independent human source, technical, pedagogy, accessibility, and answer-oracle review is required before formal-exam eligibility.

hints: Start from the stated input/output contract, implement the smallest language-specific operation, and run the visible boundary before changing structure.

feedback.correct: The visible and hidden Python: Beginner to Intermediate runner checks match the declared contract.
feedback.incorrect: At least one deterministic runner check differs; inspect input parsing, boundary behavior, and exact output before retrying.

criteria.description: Compiles or parses and passes every visible and hidden deterministic runner check for the declared contract.

privateAuthorNotes: Reference solution and both tests are AI-assisted draft material. Execute in the pinned runner and obtain independent human review before any exam use.

answer.explanation: The generator yields one bounded slice at a time and naturally handles a final partial chunk.
